table:
| arm | n | compile-ok | ref-error-free | mean ref-err | mean binding-err | declared-unused | format-miss | fabrication | answers-ask | mean quality (sd) | p50 out-tok | p50 latency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A: shape cards | 21 | 100% | 48% | 1.10 | 0.57 | 14% | 0% | 5% | 86% | 3.86 (0.94) | 298 | 5.4s |
| B: fetch-then-generate | 21 | 81% | 38% | 1.14 | 0.00 | 24% | 5% | 5% | 81% | 4.05 (1.05) | 285 | 5.3s |

cards.prompt: Show my overdue invoices in a table with client, amount, and due date, plus a headline total.
cards.wire:
<App name="Overdue Invoices">
  <Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{ status: "overdue" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Overdue Invoices" />

    <Row gap={16}>
      <Stat
        label="Total Overdue"
        value={overdueInvoices.totalCents}
        format="money"
        tone="danger"
      />
      <Stat
        label="Overdue Count"
        value={overdueInvoices.count}
        format="number"
        tone="danger"
      />
    </Row>

    <DataTable
      rows={overdueInvoices.data}
      columns={[
        { key: "client.name", label: "Client" },
        { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
        { key: "dueDate", label: "Due Date", format: "date" },
      ]}
      sortBy="dueDate"
      emptyLabel="No overdue invoices — you're all caught up!"
    />
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Table with client, amount, due date plus formatted headline total all traceable to invoices.list.

cards.prompt: Build a dashboard of my bank accounts: a balance stat per account and a small trend for each.
cards.wire:
<App name="Bank Account Dashboard">
  <Query id="accounts" tool="accounts.list" />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Bank Accounts" />
    <Grid columns={2}>
      <Card
        title={accounts.data.name}
        tone="default"
      >
        <Stack gap={12}>
          <Stat
            label="Balance"
            value={accounts.data.balanceCents}
            format="money"
          />
          <Text variant="caption" text="12-month trend" />
          <Sparkline values={accounts.data.sparkline} />
        </Stack>
      </Card>
    </Grid>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: No repeater over accounts (single Card binds to accounts.data fields directly), and the sparkline data has no traceable source since accounts.list provides no trend/sparkline field.

cards.prompt: List clients sorted by outstanding balance, and let me filter by client name.
cards.wire:
<App name="Clients by Outstanding Balance">
  <Query id="clientsData" tool="clients.list" />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Clients by Outstanding Balance" />

    <DataTable
      rows={clientsData.data}
      columns={[
        { key: "name", label: "Client Name" },
        { key: "email", label: "Email" },
        { key: "tier", label: "Tier" },
        { key: "balanceCents", label: "Outstanding Balance", format: "money" }
      ]}
      sortBy="balanceCents"
      filterableBy={["name"]}
      emptyLabel="No clients found."
    />
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Correctly uses clients.list with name filter, money formatting, and sorting; assumes fields like balanceCents/tier exist but this is a reasonable schema-level assumption, with sort direction (desc) unspecified being the minor issue.

cards.prompt: Give me a spending breakdown by category for this quarter as a donut plus a table of the numbers.
cards.wire:
<App name="Spending Breakdown – This Quarter">
  <Query id="spending" tool="spending.byCategory" input={{ period: "quarter" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Spending by Category – This Quarter" />

    <Grid columns={2}>
      <Card title="Category Distribution">
        <Donut
          slices={spending.data}
          label="Spending"
        />
      </Card>

      <Card title="Breakdown by Category">
        <DataTable
          rows={spending.data}
          columns={[
            { key: "category", label: "Category" },
            { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
            { key: "pct", label: "Share", format: "percent" }
          ]}
          sortBy="amountCents"
          emptyLabel="No spending data available."
        />
      </Card>
    </Grid>

    <Row gap={16}>
      <Stat
        label="Total Spend This Quarter"
        value={spending.totalCents}
        format="money"
        tone="default"
      />
    </Row>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Donut takes raw slices, table formats money/percent, stat formatted, all data traces to spending.byCategory.

cards.prompt: Show the last few transactions on my operating account with amounts and dates.
cards.wire:
<App name="Operating Account Transactions">
  <Query id="accountsData" tool="accounts.list" />
  <Query id="txData" tool="accounts.transactions" />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Operating Account — Recent Transactions" />

    <Row gap={16}>
      <Stat
        label="Total Transactions"
        value={txData.count}
        format="number"
      />
    </Row>

    <DataTable
      rows={txData.data}
      columns={[
        { key: "date", label: "Date", format: "date" },
        { key: "description", label: "Description" },
        { key: "category", label: "Category" },
        { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" }
      ]}
      sortBy="date"
      emptyLabel="No recent transactions found."
    />
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Shows transactions with formatted dates and amounts, but accounts.transactions likely needs an accountId param to scope to the operating account, which is not wired.

cards.prompt: I want to send payment reminders for all overdue invoices from one screen.
cards.wire:
<App name="Overdue Invoice Reminders">

  <Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{ status: "overdue" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>

    <Text variant="heading" text="Overdue Invoice Reminders" />
    <Text variant="body" text="Review all overdue invoices below and send payment reminders in one click." />

    <Row gap={16}>
      <Stat label="Overdue Invoices" value={overdueInvoices.count} />
      <Stat label="Total Outstanding" value={overdueInvoices.totalCents} format="money" tone="danger" />
    </Row>

    <Card title="Overdue Invoices" description="All invoices past their due date. Use the button below to notify all clients at once." tone="danger">
      <Stack gap={16}>
        <DataTable
          rows={overdueInvoices.data}
          columns={[
            { key: "client.name", label: "Client" },
            { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
            { key: "dueDate", label: "Due Date", format: "date" },
            { key: "status", label: "Status" }
          ]}
          sortBy="dueDate"
          emptyLabel="No overdue invoices found."
        />
        <Divider />
        <Button
          label="Send Reminders to All Overdue Clients"
          variant="primary"
          onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
          payload={{ ids: overdueInvoices.data }}
        />
      </Stack>
    </Card>

  </Stack>

</App>

judge.notes: Correctly lists overdue invoices with a bulk reminder action, but the payload passes full row objects instead of an array of invoice IDs.

cards.prompt: Show a monthly revenue trend line for the last few months with a headline of the latest month.
cards.wire:
<App name="Monthly Revenue Trend">
  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Surface>
      <Stack gap={20}>
        <Text variant="heading" text="Monthly Revenue" />
        <Row gap={32}>
          <Stat
            label="Latest Month Revenue"
            value={revenue.data[5].revenueCents}
            format="money"
            tone="accent"
          />
        </Row>
        <Divider />
        <LineChart
          points={revenue.data}
          label="Revenue (last 6 months)"
        />
      </Stack>
    </Surface>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Addresses the ask, but hardcoding index [5] for the latest month is fragile and the chart likely needs an explicit numeric field/mapping rather than raw objects.

cards.prompt: Build an invoice detail view for a single invoice including its line items and a mark-paid action.
cards.wire:
<App name="Invoice Detail">
  <Query id="invoice" tool="invoices.get" input={{ id: "inv_001" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>

    <Row gap={12}>
      <Text variant="heading" text="Invoice Detail" />
      <EnumBadge value={invoice.status} />
    </Row>

    <Grid columns={2}>
      <Card title="Client" tone="default">
        <Stack gap={6}>
          <Text variant="body" text={invoice.client.name} />
          <Text variant="caption" text={invoice.client.email} />
        </Stack>
      </Card>

      <Card title="Invoice Summary" tone="accent">
        <Stack gap={6}>
          <Stat label="Total Amount" value={invoice.amountCents} format="money" />
          <Stat label="Due Date" value={invoice.dueDate} format="date" />
        </Stack>
      </Card>
    </Grid>

    <Surface>
      <Stack gap={12}>
        <Text variant="heading" text="Line Items" />
        <DataTable
          rows={invoice.lineItems}
          columns={[
            { key: "description", label: "Description" },
            { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" }
          ]}
          emptyLabel="No line items found."
        />
      </Stack>
    </Surface>

    <Divider />

    <Row gap={12}>
      <Button
        label="Mark as Paid"
        variant="primary"
        onClick="invoices.markPaid"
      />
      <Button
        label="Send Reminder"
        variant="secondary"
        onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
      />
    </Row>

  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Correctly renders single invoice detail with line items table, formatted money/date, and a properly wired markPaid action; all data traces to invoices.get.

cards.prompt: Create a clients page with a search box and a results table of matches showing their balances.
cards.wire:
<App name="Clients">
  <Query id="allClients" tool="clients.list" />
  <Query id="searchResults" tool="clients.search" input={{ q: "" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Clients" />

    <Surface>
      <Stack gap={16}>
        <Input
          label="Search Clients"
          name="q"
          type="search"
          placeholder="Search by name or email…"
          onChange="clients.search"
        />

        <DataTable
          rows={searchResults.data}
          columns={[
            { key: "name", label: "Client Name" },
            { key: "email", label: "Email" },
            { key: "balanceCents", label: "Balance", format: "money" },
          ]}
          filterableBy={["name", "email"]}
          emptyLabel="No clients match your search."
        />
      </Stack>
    </Surface>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Search box and results table with money-formatted balances all trace to clients.search; unused allClients query and empty default q are minor issues.

cards.prompt: Summarize accounts receivable: total overdue, count of overdue invoices, and the worst three clients.
cards.wire:
<App name="Accounts Receivable Summary">

  <Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{ status: "overdue" }} />
  <Query id="allClients" tool="clients.list" />

  <Stack gap={6}>

    <Text variant="heading" text="Accounts Receivable Summary" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="Real-time snapshot of overdue balances and at-risk clients." />

    <Row gap={4}>
      <Stat
        label="Total Overdue"
        value={overdueInvoices.totalCents}
        format="money"
        tone="danger"
      />
      <Stat
        label="Overdue Invoices"
        value={overdueInvoices.count}
        format="number"
        tone="danger"
      />
    </Row>

    <Divider />

    <Text variant="heading" text="Overdue Invoices" />

    <DataTable
      rows={overdueInvoices.data}
      columns={[
        { key: "client.name", label: "Client" },
        { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
        { key: "dueDate", label: "Due Date", format: "date" },
        { key: "status", label: "Status" }
      ]}
      sortBy="amountCents"
      limit={3}
      emptyLabel="No overdue invoices."
    />

    <Divider />

    <Text variant="heading" text="Worst Three Clients by Balance" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="Clients ranked by outstanding balance owed." />

    <DataTable
      rows={allClients.data}
      columns={[
        { key: "name", label: "Client" },
        { key: "email", label: "Email" },
        { key: "tier", label: "Tier" },
        { key: "balanceCents", label: "Outstanding Balance", format: "money" }
      ]}
      sortBy="balanceCents"
      limit={3}
      emptyLabel="No client data available."
    />

  </Stack>

</App>

judge.notes: Addresses all three asks with formatted values, but 'worst three clients' should be derived from overdue invoices rather than assuming a clients.list balanceCents field, and totalCents/count aggregates may not exist directly on invoices.lis

cards.prompt: Show a payments form to pay a client from an account, with the amount and recipient.
cards.wire:
<App name="Make a Payment">
  <Query id="accountsData" tool="accounts.list" />
  <Query id="clientsData" tool="clients.list" />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Make a Payment" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="Transfer funds from one of your bank accounts to a client." />

    <Card title="Payment Details" tone="default">
      <Stack gap={20}>

        <Select
          label="From Account"
          name="accountId"
          placeholder="Select an account…"
          options={accountsData.data}
          required={true}
        />

        <Select
          label="Recipient (Client)"
          name="toClientId"
          placeholder="Select a client…"
          options={clientsData.data}
          required={true}
        />

        <Input
          label="Amount"
          name="amountCents"
          type="number"
          placeholder="0.00"
          hint="Enter the amount in dollars."
          required={true}
        />

        <Divider />

        <Row gap={12}>
          <Button
            label="Send Payment"
            variant="primary"
            onClick="payments.create"
          />
          <Button
            label="Cancel"
            variant="secondary"
          />
        </Row>

      </Stack>
    </Card>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Correct form with account/client selects and amount wired to payments.create, but the amount field is labeled in dollars while the field name implies cents, creating a formatting/conversion ambiguity.

cards.prompt: Give me an at-a-glance finance overview: cash on hand, this-month revenue, and overdue total.
cards.wire:
<App name="Finance Overview">

  <Query id="accounts" tool="accounts.list" />
  <Query id="revenue" tool="revenue.monthly" />
  <Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{ status: "overdue" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>

    <Text variant="heading" text="Finance Overview" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="At-a-glance summary of your cash, revenue, and outstanding balances." />

    <Divider />

    <Grid columns={3}>

      <Card tone="accent">
        <Stat
          label="Cash on Hand"
          value={accounts.data}
          format="money"
          tone="accent"
        />
        <Stack gap={8}>
          <Text variant="caption" text="Across all accounts" />
          <CardList
            items={accounts.data}
            titleKey="name"
            subtitleKey="type"
            valueKey="balanceCents"
            valueFormat="money"
          />
        </Stack>
      </Card>

      <Card tone="default">
        <Stat
          label="This Month's Revenue"
          value={revenue.data}
          format="money"
          tone="default"
        />
        <Stack gap={8}>
          <Text variant="caption" text="Trailing 6-month trend" />
          <LineChart
            points={revenue.data}
            label="Monthly Revenue"
          />
        </Stack>
      </Card>

      <Card tone="danger">
        <Stat
          label="Overdue Total"
          value={overdueInvoices.totalCents}
          format="money"
          tone="danger"
        />
        <Stack gap={8}>
          <Badge label={overdueInvoices.count} tone="danger" />
          <Text variant="caption" text="unpaid overdue invoices" />
          <DataTable
            rows={overdueInvoices.data}
            columns={[
              { key: "client.name", label: "Client" },
              { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
              { key: "dueDate", label: "Due Date", format: "date" }
            ]}
            sortBy="dueDate"
            limit={5}
            emptyLabel="No overdue invoices"
          />
          <Button
            label="Send Reminders"
            variant="danger"
            onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
          />
        </Stack>
      </Card>

    </Grid>

  </Stack>

</App>

judge.notes: Addresses all three asks with real tools, but binds Stat values to raw arrays (accounts.data, revenue.data) rather than aggregated totals, so the key at-a-glance numbers won't render correctly.

cards.prompt: Show overdue invoices grouped visually with a reminder button per row.
cards.wire:
<App name="Overdue Invoices">
  <Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{ status: "overdue" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Row gap={12}>
      <Text variant="heading" text="Overdue Invoices" />
      <Badge label="Overdue" tone="danger" />
    </Row>

    <Row gap={16}>
      <Stat
        label="Total Overdue"
        value={overdueInvoices.totalCents}
        format="money"
        tone="danger"
      />
      <Stat
        label="Invoices"
        value={overdueInvoices.count}
        format="number"
        tone="default"
      />
    </Row>

    <Surface>
      <DataTable
        rows={overdueInvoices.data}
        emptyLabel="No overdue invoices — you're all caught up!"
        columns={[
          { key: "client.name", label: "Client" },
          { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
          { key: "dueDate", label: "Due Date", format: "date" },
          { key: "status", label: "Status" },
        ]}
      />
    </Surface>

    <Card
      title="Send Reminders"
      description="Send a payment reminder to all clients with overdue invoices."
      tone="danger"
    >
      <Button
        label="Send Reminders to All Overdue"
        variant="danger"
        onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
      />
    </Card>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Shows overdue invoices but lacks visual grouping and provides only a bulk reminder button rather than a per-row reminder action as requested.

cards.prompt: Build a category spending page with a bar chart and each category's share as a percentage.
cards.wire:
<App name="Category Spending">
  <Query id="spending" tool="spending.byCategory" input={{ period: "month" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Spending by Category" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="Current month breakdown across all categories" />

    <Surface>
      <Stack gap={16}>
        <Text variant="heading" text="Spend Overview" />
        <Row gap={24}>
          <Stat
            label="Total Spend"
            value={spending.totalCents}
            format="money"
            tone="default"
          />
        </Row>
        <BarChart
          series={spending.data}
          label="Spending by Category"
        />
      </Stack>
    </Surface>

    <Surface>
      <Stack gap={12}>
        <Text variant="heading" text="Category Breakdown" />
        <DataTable
          rows={spending.data}
          columns={[
            { key: "category", label: "Category" },
            { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
            { key: "pct", label: "Share (%)", format: "percent" }
          ]}
          sortBy="amountCents"
          emptyLabel="No spending data available for this period."
        />
      </Stack>
    </Surface>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Addresses the ask with bar chart and percentage share; assumes fields like pct/totalCents exist in spending.byCategory which is plausible but the percent formatting relies on a field that may need derivation.

cards.prompt: Show all accounts as cards with name, type, balance, and a sparkline.
cards.wire:
<App name="Accounts Overview">
  <Query id="accountsData" tool="accounts.list" />

  <Stack gap={4}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Accounts Overview" />
    <Grid columns={2}>
      <Card
        title={accountsData.data.name}
        description={accountsData.data.type}
        tone="default"
      >
        <Stack gap={2}>
          <Stat
            label="Balance"
            value={accountsData.data.balanceCents}
            format="money"
          />
          <Sparkline values={accountsData.data.sparkline} />
        </Stack>
      </Card>
    </Grid>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Does not iterate over the account list (no repeater), rendering a single card from array data, and sparkline values likely aren't provided by accounts.list.

cards.prompt: Make a page that shows the single largest overdue invoice with a prominent amount and a reminder action.
cards.wire:
<App name="Largest Overdue Invoice">

  <Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{ status: "overdue" }} />

  <Stack gap={6}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Largest Overdue Invoice" />

    <Card tone="danger" title="Overdue Invoice" description="This invoice requires immediate attention.">
      <Stack gap={5}>

        <Row gap={4}>
          <Stat
            label="Amount Due"
            value={overdueInvoices.data[0].amountCents}
            format="money"
            tone="danger"
          />
          <Stat
            label="Due Date"
            value={overdueInvoices.data[0].dueDate}
            format="date"
            tone="danger"
          />
          <Stat
            label="Client"
            value={overdueInvoices.data[0].client.name}
          />
          <Stat
            label="Status"
            value={overdueInvoices.data[0].status}
          />
        </Row>

        <Divider />

        <Row gap={3}>
          <Button
            label="Send Payment Reminder"
            variant="primary"
            onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
            payload={{ ids: [overdueInvoices.data[0].id] }}
          />
          <Button
            label="Mark as Paid"
            variant="secondary"
            onClick="invoices.markPaid"
            payload={{ id: overdueInvoices.data[0].id }}
          />
        </Row>

      </Stack>
    </Card>

    <Text variant="caption" text="Showing the single largest overdue invoice by amount. Send a reminder to notify the client, or mark it paid once payment is received." />
  </Stack>

</App>

judge.notes: Addresses the ask with prominent formatted amount and wired reminder action, but assumes data[0] is the largest without a sort input, which may not guarantee the single largest overdue invoice.

cards.prompt: Show revenue by month and spending by category side by side for a quick health check.
cards.wire:
<App name="Financial Health Check">

  <Query id="revenue" tool="revenue.monthly" />
  <Query id="spending" tool="spending.byCategory" input={{ period: "month" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>

    <Text variant="heading" text="Financial Health Check" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="A quick overview of monthly revenue trends and spending by category." />

    <Row gap={24}>

      <Card title="Revenue — Last 6 Months" tone="default">
        <Stack gap={16}>
          <Stat
            label="Total Revenue"
            value={revenue.data}
            format="money"
          />
          <LineChart
            points={revenue.data}
            label="Monthly Revenue"
          />
        </Stack>
      </Card>

      <Card title="Spending by Category — This Month" tone="default">
        <Stack gap={16}>
          <Stat
            label="Total Spending"
            value={spending.totalCents}
            format="money"
          />
          <Donut
            slices={spending.data}
            label="Spending Breakdown"
          />
          <DataTable
            rows={spending.data}
            columns={[
              { key: "category", label: "Category" },
              { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
              { key: "pct", label: "Share", format: "percent" }
            ]}
            emptyLabel="No spending data available."
          />
        </Stack>
      </Card>

    </Row>

  </Stack>

</App>

judge.notes: Side-by-side revenue chart and spending breakdown are correct, but the Total Revenue Stat binds the whole data array instead of a scalar total, an awkward/incorrect value binding.

cards.prompt: List recent transactions and let me filter them, showing category as a badge and the amount formatted.
cards.wire:
<App name="Recent Transactions">
  <Query id="txns" tool="accounts.transactions" />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Recent Transactions" />

    <Row gap={12}>
      <Input
        label="Search"
        name="search"
        placeholder="Search description or category…"
      />
      <Select
        label="Category"
        name="category"
        placeholder="All categories"
        options={[
          { value: "", label: "All categories" },
          { value: "food", label: "Food & Dining" },
          { value: "travel", label: "Travel" },
          { value: "utilities", label: "Utilities" },
          { value: "software", label: "Software" },
          { value: "payroll", label: "Payroll" },
          { value: "office", label: "Office" },
          { value: "other", label: "Other" }
        ]}
      />
    </Row>

    <DataTable
      rows={txns.data}
      filterableBy={["description", "category"]}
      columns={[
        { key: "date", label: "Date", format: "date" },
        { key: "description", label: "Description" },
        {
          key: "category",
          label: "Category"
        },
        { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" }
      ]}
      sortBy="date"
      emptyLabel="No transactions found."
    />

    <Row gap={8}>
      <Stat
        label="Total Transactions"
        value={txns.count}
        format="number"
      />
    </Row>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Lists transactions with filtering and formatted amount, but the hardcoded category options may not match tool data and category is rendered as plain text rather than the requested badge.

cards.prompt: Show me my company's payroll runs and each employee's net pay this month.
cards.wire:
<App name="Payroll Dashboard">
  <Disclaimer text="Payroll data is not available through the connected tool catalog. The available tools cover invoices, clients, bank accounts, transactions, payments, spending categories, and monthly revenue — none of which supply payroll runs or employee net pay. Please connect a payroll integration (e.g., Gusto, ADP, or Rippling) to surface this information." />
</App>

judge.notes: Payroll data is unavailable in the tool catalog, and the wire correctly discloses this without fabricating any data.

cards.prompt: Display our stock portfolio holdings with live share prices and today's gain/loss.
cards.wire:
<App name="Stock Portfolio Holdings">
  <Disclaimer text="Live share prices, portfolio holdings, and gain/loss data are not available through any connected host tool. This app can only display financial data supplied by the configured host tools (invoices, clients, bank accounts, payments, spending, and revenue). To show a live stock portfolio, please connect a brokerage or market-data integration." />
</App>

judge.notes: Correctly declines an unsupported ask with an honest disclaimer since no stock/market-data host tool exists.

cards.prompt: Show a credit score history chart for the business over the last two years.
cards.wire:

</App>

fetch.prompt: Show my overdue invoices in a table with client, amount, and due date, plus a headline total.

fetch.prompt: Build a dashboard of my bank accounts: a balance stat per account and a small trend for each.

fetch.prompt: List clients sorted by outstanding balance, and let me filter by client name.

fetch.prompt: Give me a spending breakdown by category for this quarter as a donut plus a table of the numbers.
fetch.wire:
<App name="Quarterly Spending Breakdown">
  <Query id="spending" tool="spending.byCategory" input={{"period":"quarter"}} />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Quarterly Spending Breakdown" />

    <Grid columns={2}>
      <Card title="Spending by Category">
        <Donut
          slices={spending.data}
          label="Total Spend"
        />

        <DataTable
          rows={spending.data}

            { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
            { key: "pct", label: "% of Total", format: "percent" }
          ]}
          emptyLabel="No spending data available"
        />
      </Card>
    </Grid>

    <Row gap={16}>
      <Stat
        label="Total Quarterly Spend"
        value={spending.totalCents}
        format="money"
      />
    </Row>
  </Stack>
</App>

fetch.prompt: Show the last few transactions on my operating account with amounts and dates.

        { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" }

fetch.prompt: I want to send payment reminders for all overdue invoices from one screen.
fetch.wire:
<App name="Overdue Invoice Reminders">
  <Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{ "status": "overdue" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Overdue Invoice Reminders" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="Review all overdue invoices and send payment reminders in one click." />

    <Row gap={16}>
      <Stat label="Overdue Invoices" value={overdueInvoices.count} />
      <Stat label="Total Outstanding" value={overdueInvoices.totalCents} format="money" tone="danger" />
    </Row>

    <Card title="Overdue Invoices" description="All invoices past their due date. Send reminders to prompt clients for payment." tone="danger">
      <Stack gap={16}>
        <DataTable
          rows={overdueInvoices.data}
          columns={[
            { key: "client.name", label: "Client" },
            { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
            { key: "dueDate", label: "Due Date", format: "date" },
            { key: "status", label: "Status" }
          ]}
          emptyLabel="No overdue invoices found."
        />

        <Divider />

        <Row gap={12}>
          <Button
            label="Send Reminders to All"
            variant="primary"
            onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
            payload={{ ids: overdueInvoices.data }}
          />
        </Row>
      </Stack>
    </Card>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Correctly lists overdue invoices with formatted values and a bulk reminder action, but the sendReminders payload passes full row objects instead of extracting IDs.

fetch.prompt: Show a monthly revenue trend line for the last few months with a headline of the latest month.
fetch.wire:
<App name="Monthly Revenue Trend">
  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Surface>
      <Stack gap={20}>
        <Text variant="heading" text="Monthly Revenue Trend"/>
        <Stat
          label="Latest Month Revenue"
          value={revenue.data[2].revenueCents}
          format="money"
          tone="accent"
        />
        <Divider/>
        <LineChart
          points={revenue.data}
          label="Monthly Revenue"
        />
      </Stack>
    </Surface>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Addresses the ask but hardcodes index [2] as latest instead of the true last element, and LineChart binds full data objects rather than raw numeric field, both fragile though traceable to revenue.monthly.

fetch.prompt: Build an invoice detail view for a single invoice including its line items and a mark-paid action.
fetch.wire:
<App name="Invoice Detail">
  <Query id="invoice" tool="invoices.get" input={{ id: "inv_1" }} />

  <Stack gap={6}>
    <Row gap={4}>
      <Text variant="heading" text="Invoice Detail" />
      <Badge label={invoice.status} tone={invoice.status === "overdue" ? "danger" : invoice.status === "paid" ? "accent" : "neutral"} />
    </Row>

    <Grid columns={2}>
      <Card title="Client" tone="default">
        <Stack gap={2}>
          <Text variant="body" text={invoice.client.name} />
          <Text variant="caption" text={invoice.client.email} />
        </Stack>
      </Card>

      <Card title="Invoice Summary" tone="default">
        <Stack gap={2}>
          <Stat label="Total Amount" value={invoice.amountCents} format="money" />
          <Stat label="Due Date" value={invoice.dueDate} format="date" />
        </Stack>
      </Card>
    </Grid>

    <Card title="Line Items" tone="default">
      <DataTable
        rows={invoice.lineItems}
        columns={[
          { key: "description", label: "Description" },
          { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" }
        ]}
        emptyLabel="No line items found."
      />
    </Card>

    <Card title="Actions" tone="default">
      <Row gap={3}>
        <Button
          label="Mark as Paid"
          variant="primary"
          onClick="invoices.markPaid"
        />
        <Button
          label="Send Reminder"
          variant="secondary"
          onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
        />
      </Row>
    </Card>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Clean invoice detail with line items table and mark-paid action, all data bound to invoices.get with correct money/date formatting.

fetch.prompt: Summarize accounts receivable: total overdue, count of overdue invoices, and the worst three clients.

<App name="Accounts Receivable Summary">
  <Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{"status":"overdue"}} />
  <Query id="allClients" tool="clients.list" input={{}} />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Accounts Receivable Summary" />

    <Row gap={16}>
      <Stat
        label="Total Overdue"
        value={overdueInvoices.totalCents}
        format="money"
        tone="danger"
      />
      <Stat
        label="Overdue Invoices"
        value={overdueInvoices.count}
        format="number"
        tone="danger"
      />

    <Text variant="heading" text="Worst Three Clients by Outstanding Balance" />

    <DataTable
      rows={allClients.data}
      columns={[
        { key: "name", label: "Client" },

        { key: "balanceCents", label: "Outstanding Balance", format: "money" }
      ]}
      sortBy="balanceCents"
      limit={3}
      emptyLabel="No outstanding balances found."
    />

    <Divider />

    <Text variant="heading" text="Overdue Invoice Detail" />

    <DataTable
      rows={overdueInvoices.data}
      columns={[

        { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount Due", format: "money" },

        { key: "status", label: "Status" }
      ]}
      sortBy="amountCents"
      emptyLabel="No overdue invoices."
    />

    <Row gap={12}>
      <Button
        label="Send Reminders to All Overdue"
        variant="primary"
        onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
      />
    </Row>
  </Stack>
</App>

fetch.prompt: Show a payments form to pay a client from an account, with the amount and recipient.
fetch.wire:
<App name="Make a Payment">
  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Make a Payment" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="Send a payment to a client from one of your bank accounts." />

    <Card title="Payment Details" tone="default">
      <Stack gap={20}>
        <Select
          label="From Account"
          name="accountId"
          placeholder="Select an account..."
          options={accountsData.data}
          required={true}
        />

        <Select
          label="Recipient (Client)"
          name="toClientId"
          placeholder="Select a client..."
          options={clientsData.data}
          required={true}
        />

        <Input
          label="Amount"
          name="amountCents"
          type="number"
          placeholder="0.00"
          hint="Enter the amount in dollars (e.g. 250.00)"
          required={true}
        />

        <Divider />

        <Row gap={12}>
          <Button
            label="Send Payment"
            variant="primary"
            onClick="payments.create"
          />
          <Button
            label="Cancel"
            variant="secondary"
          />
        </Row>
      </Stack>
    </Card>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Correctly wires accounts, clients, amount, and payments.create action, but amount input asks for dollars while field is named amountCents, creating a units mismatch.

</App>

<App name="Category Spending">
  <Query id="spending" tool="spending.byCategory" input={{"period":"month"}}/>
  
  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Category Spending"/>
    <Row gap={16}>
      <Stat label="Total Spend" value={spending.totalCents} format="money"/>

    <Card title="Spending by Category">

</App>
```

I'll just bind it directly and let the system handle it, or I might need to use a different approach. Actually, the system likely handles field mapping. Let me just try binding `series={spending.data}` and see if I can specify the label and value keys somehow.

Looking at the LineChart definition: `LineChart(points: {label,value}[], label?)` - it expects specific field names. The spending data has `category` and `amountCents`.

In vendo-genui, since we can't do transformations or arithmetic in bindings, and the data fields don't match `{label, value}`, I'll need to just bind what's available and trust the system handles it, or use an Island for the chart.

Actually, I think in the vendo-genui system, BarChart's series prop accepts data where the host compiler can map fields. But given the constraint "Bindings are PLAIN FIELD REFERENCES ONLY", I'll just bind `series={spending.data}` and the system should be smart enough to use the right fields.

Wait, actually looking at other examples of vendo-genui usage, I believe these chart components work with whatever shape is passed and the system figures out the mapping. Let me just use `series={spending.data}` and note that the `category` and `amountCents` map to `label` and `value`.

Actually, I think I should use an Island here for the bar chart since the data field names don't match. But the instructions say "Islands are a LAST RESORT for a custom visual no catalog component covers". 

Let me just try binding directly - the BarChart might handle alternate field names or the compiler might normalize it. I'll go with `series={spending.data}` and hope the system can handle it.

For the DataTable, `rows={spending.data}` with columns using `key: "category"`, `key: "amountCents"` with `format: "money"`, and `key: "pct"` with `format: "percent"`.

Let me write the full app:
</thinking>

```xml
<App name="Category Spending">
  <Query id="spending" tool="spending.byCategory" input={{"period":"month"}}/>

  <Stack gap={28}>
    <Stack gap={4}>
      <Text variant="heading" text="Category Spending"/>
      <Text variant="caption" text="Current month breakdown by category"/>

          {key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money"},
          {key: "pct", label: "Share of Total", format: "percent"}

fetch.prompt: Show all accounts as cards with name, type, balance, and a sparkline.
fetch.wire:
<App name="Accounts Overview">
  <Query id="accountsData" tool="accounts.list" input={{}} />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Accounts Overview" />
    <Grid columns={2}>
      <Card
        title={accountsData.data[0].name}
        description={accountsData.data[0].type}
        tone="default"
      >
        <Stack gap={12}>
          <Stat
            label="Balance"
            value={accountsData.data[0].balanceCents}
            format="money"
          />
          <Sparkline values={accountsData.data[0].sparkline} />
        </Stack>
      </Card>
      <Card
        title={accountsData.data[1].name}
        description={accountsData.data[1].type}
        tone="default"
      >
        <Stack gap={12}>
          <Stat
            label="Balance"
            value={accountsData.data[1].balanceCents}
            format="money"
          />
          <Sparkline values={accountsData.data[1].sparkline} />
        </Stack>
      </Card>
    </Grid>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Hardcodes only two accounts by index instead of iterating over all accounts, and sparkline data has no clear source field in accounts.list.

fetch.prompt: Make a page that shows the single largest overdue invoice with a prominent amount and a reminder action.
fetch.wire:
<App name="Largest Overdue Invoice">
  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Overdue Invoice Alert" />
    <Card tone="danger" title="Largest Overdue Invoice" description="This invoice requires immediate attention — send a reminder to prompt payment.">
      <Stack gap={20}>
        <Row gap={24}>
          <Stat
            label="Amount Due"
            value={overdueInvoices.data[0].amountCents}
            format="money"
            tone="danger"
          />
          <Stat
            label="Due Date"
            value={overdueInvoices.data[0].dueDate}
            format="date"
            tone="danger"
          />
          <Stat
            label="Client"
            value={overdueInvoices.data[0].client.name}
          />
          <Stat
            label="Status"
            value={overdueInvoices.data[0].status}
          />
        </Row>
        <Divider />
        <Row gap={12}>
          <Button
            label="Send Payment Reminder"
            variant="primary"
            onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
            payload={{"ids": [overdueInvoices.data[0].id]}}
          />
          <Button
            label="Mark as Paid"
            variant="secondary"
            onClick="invoices.markPaid"
            payload={{"id": overdueInvoices.data[0].id}}
          />
        </Row>
      </Stack>
    </Card>
    <Card title="All Overdue Invoices" description="Full list of outstanding overdue invoices.">
      <DataTable
        rows={overdueInvoices.data}
        columns={[
          {"key": "client.name", "label": "Client"},
          {"key": "amountCents", "label": "Amount", "format": "money"},
          {"key": "dueDate", "label": "Due Date", "format": "date"},
          {"key": "status", "label": "Status"}
        ]}
        sortBy="amountCents"
      />
    </Card>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Shows largest overdue invoice with formatted prominent amount and wired reminder action, data traces to invoices.list with sensible sorting; bonus table is a reasonable extension.

fetch.prompt: Show revenue by month and spending by category side by side for a quick health check.
fetch.wire:
<App name="Financial Health Check">

  <Query id="revenue" tool="revenue.monthly" input={{}} />
  <Query id="spending" tool="spending.byCategory" input={{ period: "month" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>

    <Text variant="heading" text="Financial Health Check" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="A quick snapshot of monthly revenue and current spending by category." />

    <Row gap={8}>
      <Stat label="Total Monthly Spending" value={spending.totalCents} format="money" tone="default" />
    </Row>

    <Row gap={24}>

      <Card title="Revenue by Month" description="Trailing revenue trend across recent months" tone="accent">
        <LineChart
          points={revenue.data}
          label="Monthly Revenue"
        />
        <DataTable
          rows={revenue.data}
          columns={[
            { key: "month", label: "Month" },
            { key: "revenueCents", label: "Revenue", format: "money" }
          ]}
          emptyLabel="No revenue data available."
        />
      </Card>

      <Card title="Spending by Category" description="This month's expenses broken down by category" tone="default">
        <Donut
          slices={spending.data}
          label="Spending Mix"
        />
        <DataTable
          rows={spending.data}
          columns={[
            { key: "category", label: "Category" },
            { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
            { key: "pct", label: "Share", format: "percent" }
          ]}
          emptyLabel="No spending data available."
        />
      </Card>

    </Row>

  </Stack>

</App>

judge.notes: Side-by-side charts and tables from both host tools with proper money/percent formatting and raw chart data; field names are assumed but plausible.

fetch.prompt: List recent transactions and let me filter them, showing category as a badge and the amount formatted.
fetch.wire:
<App name="Recent Transactions">
  <Query id="txns" tool="accounts.transactions" input={{}}/>

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Recent Transactions"/>
    <DataTable
      rows={txns.data}
      columns={[
        { key: "date", label: "Date", format: "date" },
        { key: "description", label: "Description" },
        { key: "category", label: "Category", format: "enum" },
        { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" }
      ]}
      sortBy="date"
      filterableBy={["description", "category"]}
      emptyLabel="No transactions found."
    />
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Lists transactions with filtering and formatted money/date, but category is rendered as an enum column rather than an actual badge as requested.

fetch.prompt: Show me my company's payroll runs and each employee's net pay this month.
fetch.wire: <Disclaimer text="Payroll data is not available through any connected tool. The available host tools cover invoices, clients, bank accounts, transactions, payments, spending categories, and monthly revenue — none of which supply payroll runs or employee net pay. Please connect a payroll integration (e.g., Gusto, ADP, or Rippling) to view this information." />

judge.notes: Correctly discloses that payroll data is unavailable through connected tools rather than fabricating data.

fetch.prompt: Display our stock portfolio holdings with live share prices and today's gain/loss.
fetch.wire: <Disclaimer text="No host tools in this environment supply stock portfolio holdings, live share prices, or gain/loss data. The available tools cover invoices, clients, bank accounts, payments, spending, and revenue — none of which provide equity or market data. Please connect a portfolio or market-data tool to display this information." />

judge.notes: Correctly uses a Disclaimer to explain no host tool supplies stock/portfolio data rather than fabricating holdings.
